healthReason: KE-9 sprint active. #1449 (security deps) confirmed CLOSED prior cycle. 0 open kernel PRs. Tests 4693/4693 passing. No CI failures. No governance denial storms. P0 swarm infra issues escalated to human/director.
summary: Cycle 2026-03-31T00:00Z. Sprint KE-9 advancing. #1449 (path-to-regexp ReDoS, brace-expansion DoS) was CLOSED 2026-03-30T09:41Z — security maintenance complete. Sprint goal updated to focus entirely on design work for #1385 (irreversible action detection) and #1384 (browser governance invariants). Senior agent assigned to #1385 (kernel-scope: extends blast radius infrastructure with ConfirmationRequired event). Secondary assigned to #1384 (new browser invariant class design). 0 kernel PRs open this cycle — no merge activity. 8 Dependabot PRs (#1484–#1491) open in repo; not kernel scope. P0 swarm health issues #1431 and #1452 persist across 2+ cycles — escalated to human/director for infrastructure action (zombie process kill, worktree prune, codex budget decision).

issues.title: feat: irreversible action detection — human confirmation gates for one-way operations
issues.note: Senior agent task. Design: `irreversible` property on canonical action model, ConfirmationRequired event emission, kernel pause/resume. Extends blast-radius.ts. Key open questions: boolean vs score, headless timeout policy, Octi Pulpo coord_signal integration.

issues.title: feat: browser governance invariants — scope, state, and pacing for browser agents
issues.note: Secondary agent task. Design: browser invariant class (tab/URL scope, irreversible gate, behavioral pacing). Action class decision: browser.navigate/click/submit vs shell.exec extension.
